danger_signals.visual_form: Bullet patterns emanating from enemy units; density increases with stage progression
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat to player; denser patterns indicate higher difficulty/danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Tanks, battleships, kamikaze monoplanes, artillery, zeppelins rendered on scrolling battlefield
danger_signals.meaning: Armed hostiles requiring destruction or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Player ship sprite reverts to basic weapon configuration after hit
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state—player has taken damage and lost upgraded weapons

danger_signals.visual_form: Player craft disappears from screen; life counter decrements
danger_signals.meaning: Permanent failure state if all lives exhausted; signals game-over risk

safety_signals.visual_form: Aircraft landing zone at end of scrolling sequence; stage transition
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; progression milestone; respawn point (checkpoint) in arcade version

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored icon drops from destroyed zeppelins (wide shot, laser, flamethrower)
safety_signals.meaning: Upgrades to offensive capability; enhance safety through firepower
Enhancement resources; increased survivability and scoring

safety_signals.visual_form: Terrain/water layers scroll downward; player always progresses forward
safety_signals.meaning: Forward momentum = progress toward safety; stalled motion = danger

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Airport/runway locations; safe zones at stage endpoints

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military hardware aesthetic: tanks, battleships, monoplanes, artillery, zeppelins
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unit type variation (ground vehicles, naval, air); kamikaze monoplanes indicate fanatical enemy doctrine
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Island base in Mediterranean (arcade); industrial/military terrain in Genesis version

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Global threat emerging (arcade: Strange Fleet arrival; Genesis: S Corps invasion)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasingly dense enemy spawns across ten stages; military infrastructure dominates landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy fire patterns intensify; unit density increases per stage; respawn rates accelerate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scrolling battlefield with active enemy units and projectiles; no safe terrain until airport
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant forward motion; unavoidable enemy encounters; must destroy or evade

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Airport/runway appears; scrolling halts; stage transition screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can land safely; firepower/speed reset occurs if damaged; loop begins next stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same ten stages repeat but with denser enemy fire patterns and unit placement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy bullets increase in volume and complexity; difficulty curve visible in sprite/projectile density

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through landscape filled with tanks, battleships, kamikaze aircraft tells story of organized military invasion without dialogue
Scrolling reveals different combat zones (island, industrial areas, water) implying geographic scope of conflict
Takeoff sequence with drawn crowd establishes civilian stakes and public support for player's mission
Airports as islands of respite within hostile territory reinforce 'resistance base' narrative
Zeppelin destruction yielding upgraded weapons (laser, flamethrower) implies enemy tech repurposing by player faction

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire projectiles in fixed patterns; move predictably on terrain; destroyed by player fire
Aggressive flight patterns; suggest fanatical enemy doctrine; high threat value
Stationary or slow-moving; high firepower output; dense projectile patterns indicate major threat
Spawn weapon pickups when destroyed; prioritized targets for power progression
Maximum firepower output; widest weapon spread; highest survivability; icon grabs yield points instead
Firepower and speed reset to base; visual downgrade (sprite reverts); vulnerability window until re-powered

notes: Fire Shark uses minimal narrative exposition, relying heavily on visual language to communicate world-state. The arcade version establishes a mysterious armada threat through environmental density and progression, while the Genesis port reframes the conflict as corporate/industrial domination (S Corps). Both versions use scrolling-shooter conventions: safe zones (airports) contrast with hostile terrain, weapon pickup colors indicate upgrade type, and enemy variety signals faction identity and threat level. The game communicates difficulty increase purely through visual density (enemy count, projectile volume) rather than dialogue or text—a hallmark of arcade shmup design. Stage looping with increased difficulty is communicated through identical terrain with denser hazards, creating a 'rising tide' world-state narrative.
